title: Global search overlay component with keyboard navigation and result handling
purpose: Provides a cross-artifact search overlay accessible via Cmd+K/Ctrl+K that searches pseudo files and linked code snippets with unified results and navigation.
module_context: Exports a GlobalSearch React component that manages search state, handles keyboard shortcuts, debounces search requests, and renders a searchable overlay with result highlighting and navigation.

steps.content: Return an empty string if the input path is empty.
Split the path by '/' and return the last segment as the basename.
Return an empty string if the file path is null or undefined.
Extract the basename from the file path.
Find the last dot in the basename.
If a dot exists and is not at the start, return the substring before the dot.
Otherwise return the basename as-is.
Attempt to parse the snippet content as JSON.
IF the parsed content has a filePath property, return it.
ELSE check if the snippet has a linked property and a filePath metadata.
IF the snippet is linked and has filePath metadata, return the metadata path.
ELSE return the snippet's filePath metadata or null if not present.
Check if the snippet has a linked property set to true.
IF not, attempt to parse the snippet content as JSON.
IF the parsed content has a linked property set to true, return true.
ELSE return false.
Filter the snippets to only include those marked as linked.
Look for an exact match of the file path in the linked snippets.
IF an exact match is found, return it.
ELSE extract the file stem from the target file path.
IF no file stem can be extracted, return null.
ELSE search for a linked snippet with a matching file stem.
Return the matching snippet or null if none found.
Open the global search overlay in the store.
Reset the search query to an empty string.
Clear the search results list.
Reset the error message to null.
Reset the selected index to zero.
Close the global search overlay in the store.
Reset the search query to an empty string.
Clear the search results list.
Reset the error message to null.
Reset the selected index to zero.
Clear any pending debounce timeout if present.
Listen for keyboard events on the document.
IF the Cmd or Ctrl key is pressed with K key, prevent default behavior.
IF the overlay is already open, close it.
ELSE open the overlay.
If the overlay is open, set a timeout to focus the input element.
Clean up the timeout on component unmount.
If the overlay is not open or no session is available, return early.
Trim the search query and return if empty.
Clear any existing debounce timeout.
Set a new debounce timeout to execute the search after 200ms.
Increment the request ID for tracking.
Set loading state to true and clear any previous error.
Execute the code search API call with the project, session, and query.
IF the request ID has changed, ignore the response.
Update the results list with the API response.
Reset the selected index to zero.
IF the API call fails, update the error message and clear results.
Reset loading state after the API call completes.
If the results list reference is not set, return early.
Find the DOM element corresponding to the selected index.
Scroll the selected element into view with nearest block alignment.
Set the pending jump state with the snippet ID and line number.
Select the snippet in the session store.
Close the global search overlay.
IF the result is of code kind, jump to the snippet.
ELSE find an existing linked snippet for the file path.
IF an existing snippet is found, jump to it.
ELSE prepare a link candidate for the new snippet.
Set the link dialog state to open with the candidate and symbol name.
Set the target line number for the link.
Open the link and navigation dialog.
IF no session or link candidate is available, return early.
Link the file using the project and session information.
Set the pending jump state with the new snippet ID and line number.
Select the new snippet in the session store.
Close the link dialog and reset link candidate state.
Close the global search overlay.
IF the Escape key is pressed, prevent default and close the overlay.
IF the Down arrow key is pressed, prevent default and move selection down.
IF the Up arrow key is pressed, prevent default and move selection up.
IF the Enter key is pressed, prevent default and handle the selected result.
